Murielle, you present to the board tomorrow. Give me your summary.
Overall, turnover is lower than last year, and the North is our best region.
We have many strong teams but little budget. Last quarter we were losing staff fast.
We should invest in the North, and we could expand the mentoring program.
Nice. Can you make it more persuasive?
Yes. My main takeaway is clear: from a data standpoint, we should act now.
Last quarter we were losing our best analysts.

Good morning, board members. Let me summarize this year's HR results. Overall, our turnover is lower than last year, and the North is our strongest region. We have many talented teams, but our budget is minimal. Last quarter, we were losing our best analysts, which was our biggest risk. From an HR standpoint, the message is clear. I want to highlight two decisions. First, we should invest more in the North. Second, we could expand the mentoring program. My main takeaway is simple: we should act now, and I am confident the results will be better next year. Thank you.

Hello everyone. I want to present our decision clearly and concisely. This year was our most challenging, but also our most successful. We had far more applications than last year, yet fewer recruiters. While we were struggling with the workload, our engagement scores were rising. That is remarkable. Overall, the North performed best, and the South made the most progress. From a strategic standpoint, we should keep our current plan, but we could add one more recruiter. My final takeaway is coherent and simple: our people are our greatest strength, and we should invest in them. Thank you for listening.
